concept: Two NOAA AI systems, a robot EAR (open-source deep-learning passive acoustics) and a robot EYE (GAIA satellite computer vision), now watch Cook Inlet's endangered belugas off Anchorage. We see the whale through the machines. The earned turn: watching is not saving. What the AI really hands Alaska is one hard, open number, 331, and whether that becomes a floor to defend or just a countdown on record is a human choice.

angle.thesis: Two fresh NOAA AI systems can finally find Cook Inlet's 331 belugas by ear AND by eye at once; that stereo-sensing capability is a genuine Alaska-first win, but a sharper count is not a rescue, and what Alaska does with the number decides whether 331 is a floor or a countdown.
angle.fair_counter: Better monitoring does NOT by itself reverse the decline; NOAA attributes the endangered status to ocean noise, prey, habitat, contaminants, predation, strandings.

fingerprint.palette: silty pewter water, ice-white beluga, mint sonar wash, signal-gold lock, ink shadow
fingerprint.metaphor: the rarest whale watched through a machine's ear and eye at once

palette: silty pewter water, ice-white beluga, mint sonar wash, signal-gold lock, ink shadow

light.sun: flat overcast north light from above the surface; the sensors are their own emissive sources
light.mood: cold and clear and alert; the only sharp man-made light in a soft natural world is the machine's

hook.frame1: the ice-white beluga already surfacing, silty water, bold headline present at frame 0
hook.headline: TWO EYES ON THE LAST WHALES
hook.loopback: S9 returns to this exact surfacing whale with both sensors still pulsing

reveals.what: the lone 331 pulls back to reveal the ghost pod of the ~1,000 missing belugas
the reticle box morphs into the data card holding the number
the two sensor overlays build onto the whale

shots.centered: the beluga surfacing as both sensor overlays lock onto it

hero.name: Beluga
hero.detail_note: bulbous melon, smile, blowhole spout, flukes, pectoral fins; zones white lit back / gray belly / mint sensor rim

shots.centered: the sea-and-sky cutaway: mooring below, satellite above

hero.name: ListeningMooring
hero.detail_note: anchor base, ribbed cylinder, hydrophone dome, tether float; gunmetal body / mint sensor face / rope. NET-NEW, emits SmellRings as sonar

shots.centered: the seafloor mooring, sonar rings blooming as it catches a call

hero.name: ListeningMooring
hero.detail_note: anchor base, ribbed cylinder, hydrophone dome, tether float; gunmetal body / mint sensor face / rope. NET-NEW, emits SmellRings as sonar

shots.centered: the reticle clamping the white smudge that resolves into a whale

hero.name: SatelliteEye
hero.detail_note: angular bus, twin solar wings, lens barrel, comms dish, telemetry light; gunmetal hull / gold lens / dark cells. NET-NEW, projects ScanReticle

shots.centered: the number 331 landing on the readout
shots.means: the reticle box becomes the count
shots.stage3d: flat:a legible readout screen; depth adds nothing to a single number

hero.name: CountReadout
hero.detail_note: bezel, glowing 331 digits, label plate, status LED; emissive screen / form-shaded bezel / label

shots.centered: the decline bar dropping 1,300 to 279 to 331 with the ghost pod

hero.name: DeclineBar
hero.detail_note: tall 1979 bar, short present bar, ghost-pod silhouettes, baseline axis; solid ink bar / translucent ghost fill / labels

shots.centered: the whale swimming while the HUD pulses uselessly

hero.name: Beluga
hero.detail_note: bulbous melon, smile, blowhole spout, flukes, pectoral fins; zones white lit back / gray belly / mint sensor rim

shots.centered: 331 as a public, timestamped, open-source fact
shots.stage3d: flat:a public readout screen; depth adds nothing to the open fact

hero.name: CountReadout
hero.detail_note: bezel, glowing 331 digits, label plate, status LED; emissive screen / form-shaded bezel / label

shots.centered: 331 forks into floor vs countdown; the whale dives; raven caws

hero.name: Beluga
hero.detail_note: bulbous melon, smile, blowhole spout, flukes, pectoral fins; zones white lit back / gray belly / mint sensor rim

beats.vo: Off Anchorage swims one of the rarest whales on Earth.

draw.subject: an ice-white beluga with a soft smile in silty pewter Cook Inlet water
draw.action: rises and blows a small misty spout
draw.annotation: TWO EYES ON THE LAST WHALES

choreo.primary: the whale arcs up to the surface
choreo.ambient: silt motes drift, faint Anchorage skyline behind

draw.subject: a mint sonar ring and a gold targeting reticle
draw.action: snap onto the whale's call and body at the same instant

choreo.primary: the reticle clamps shut on the whale
choreo.reaction: the whale's rim lights mint as the ring lands

beats.vo: NOAA switched on two AI systems

draw.subject: a sea-and-sky cutaway: a listening mooring on the seafloor, a satellite in orbit above
draw.action: both power on, telemetry lights blinking awake

choreo.primary: the two machines light up in sequence
choreo.ambient: god-rays waver down through the water

draw.subject: the two machines with the rounded whale between them for scale
draw.action: turn to face the whale from below and above

choreo.primary: the machines pivot toward the whale
choreo.reaction: the whale glides calmly between them

draw.subject: the seafloor ListeningMooring with a hydrophone dome
draw.action: blooms concentric mint sonar rings as it catches a single call

choreo.primary: rings bloom outward from the dome
choreo.reaction: the tether float bobs on the pulse

beats.vo: A deep learning model reads the noisy inlet and finds a single beluga call.
beats.shows: the call waveform boxed and labeled

draw.subject: a green call waveform on the mooring's readout
draw.action: spikes, gets boxed DETECT, then labeled BELUGA

choreo.primary: the waveform spikes and a box snaps around it

draw.subject: the SatelliteEye high above a cloud deck
draw.action: punches its scan cone down through the clouds to the silty inlet

beats.vo: GAIA scans satellite photos from orbit

draw.subject: the gold ScanReticle sweeping over the inlet surface
draw.action: spins wide, hunting across the silty water

choreo.primary: the reticle spins and drifts searching

beats.vo: for the white smudge of a whale.

draw.subject: the gold ScanReticle over a tiny white smudge
draw.action: clamps tight as the smudge resolves into a beluga

choreo.primary: the reticle snaps inward and locks
choreo.reaction: an impact star bursts at the lock

beats.vo: Ear and eye, the same whale, at once, for the first time.

draw.subject: the whale flanked by the mint ring and the gold box together
draw.action: both overlays hold on the one animal at once

choreo.primary: the two overlays breathe in unison on the whale
choreo.reaction: the whale gives a slow tail stroke

beats.vo: It matters because only about 331 of these belugas are left.

choreo.primary: the digits roll up and land on 331

beats.sfx: counter riser to a soft hit
beats.rehook: the count climbs and lands on 331, re-grabbing the viewer with how few remain

draw.subject: a tall bar of belugas beside a short one
draw.action: drops from 1,300 down to 279 to 331 as a ghost pod fades in behind

choreo.reaction: the ghost pod of missing whales fades in behind

draw.subject: a bold percentage stamp over the ghost pod
draw.action: slams down across the missing whales

choreo.primary: the stamp slams onto the ghost pod
choreo.reaction: the ghost whales shudder on impact

beats.shows: the whale under a boat wake

draw.subject: the beluga swimming as the mint ring and gold box keep pulsing
draw.action: a loud boat wake rolls over it while the sensors glow on, useless

choreo.primary: the boat wake rolls across the frame over the whale
choreo.reaction: the whale flinches but the HUD keeps pulsing

beats.vo: Sharper eyes do not quiet the boats or refill the salmon these whales need.

draw.subject: labels for the real threats the sensors cannot touch
draw.action: drift past the glowing HUD: noise, lost salmon, habitat

choreo.primary: threat labels drift through and past the reticle
choreo.ambient: the glow dims as they pass

beats.vo: What the AI gives Alaska is one hard, open number. 331.

draw.subject: the number 331 on an open, timestamped readout with an OPEN-SOURCE tag
draw.action: locks in as a hard public fact, cursor blinking

choreo.primary: the number keys in and locks with a confirm
choreo.reaction: an OPEN-SOURCE tag stamps beside it

beats.vo: Whether that becomes a floor to defend,

draw.subject: the number 331 forking into a flat floor line and a downward countdown
draw.action: the whale rests on the floor line as the countdown ticks below

choreo.primary: the number splits into two diverging paths
choreo.reaction: the whale settles onto the floor line
choreo.ambient: the countdown digits tick down below

beats.vo: or just a countdown on record, no machine decides.

draw.subject: the cold-open whale diving with both sensors still pulsing, a raven perched in the corner
draw.action: slips under as the raven cocks its head and caws once

choreo.primary: the whale dives out of frame
choreo.reaction: the raven tilts its head and caws
choreo.ambient: both sensors keep pulsing on the empty water

rehook.what: the decline bar collapsing behind the 331 and the ghost pod fading in re-grabs a sagging viewer

divergence_note: This board rotates hard off the last two dispatches. 07-21 Pen was an eye-level classroom drama (instrument-as-subject, centered-hero-lowerthird, vertical-descent, slate-indigo dusk + copper); 07-21 salmon was an eye-level river two-world (multiplicity-swarm, fullbleed-split, horizontal-traverse, bone-tan/glacial). This one is seen THROUGH the machine (instrument-screen), framed as a sensor HUD (hud-instrument-frame), moving by radial scan/sonar emanation, instrument-readout register, silty pewter + mint + gold palette sharing no tokens with either. Hero rotates from ServerMachine to the Beluga. Spatial signature (instrument-screen, hud-instrument-frame, radial-emanate) unique vs the last four.
